title: Luma Framework manifest
description: Overrides + catalogue manifest for Luma Framework installs. Carries NO payload hashes/sizes: the add-on is fetched live from a single upstream rolling GitHub Release (Filoppi/Luma-Framework), so this document maps games to a release asset file name + match rules + per-game overrides, plus Luma's minimum ReShade host version and shared title defaults. ReShade download sources live in the standalone reshade_manifest.json. Deliberately narrower than the RenoDX manifest: there is no top-level `generics` fallback list (every Generic-Mod-compatible game is an explicit title with `generic: true`), no `external`/`native_hdr` category (every asset lives on the same GitHub Release), and no `slug`/`download_url`/`compatibility` fields. The Rust validator in renderpilot-orchestration (luma::parse_manifest) is the canonical gatekeeper; this schema mirrors it for editor tooling and CI.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp, date-only granularity (T00:00:00Z). Honors SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH for reproducible builds.

min_reshade_version.description: Minimum ReShade host version Luma's current builds require when RenderPilot considers reusing an already-present host. ReShade download URLs are intentionally not part of the Luma manifest.

defaults.description: Shared title defaults. The app applies these to every title that omits the corresponding field; the generator only emits a per-title field when it differs from these.

titles.description: The single catalogue of every game Luma is known to work with. Each entry is matched by its rules and optionally routed by `category` (blacklist). Omitted category means installable.

asset.description: GitHub Release asset file name (e.g. Luma-Dishonored_2.zip, Luma-Unreal_Engine.zip). A `-x32` suffix must agree with `arch: X86`. `-Test`/`-Dev` build markers are curated out (Publishing assets only).

category.description: How a matched game is routed. Omitted = installable (a normal Luma install). Deliberately narrower than RenoDX's category: every Luma asset lives on the same GitHub Release, so there is no `external`; a native-HDR title is simply absent from the catalogue rather than modelled.

reason.description: i18n key explaining why (known-broken, or requires an external prerequisite this installer doesn't automate, e.g. Vanquish's dgVoodoo).

externalRequirement.description: Managed installer-owned dependency that RenderPilot downloads, verifies, installs, and configures. The matcher may accept the listed detected APIs for this title and install the ReShade host into the declared proxy DLL slot.

reshade_proxy_dll.description: ReShade host proxy DLL slot (e.g. dxgi.dll). Distinct from dgVoodoo's own wrapper DLL (e.g. D3D9.dll), which is declared in install_map.

source.description: Managed archive source for downloading and verifying the dependency.

install_map.description: Archive path -> game directory path mapping for files extracted from the dependency archive.

config.description: Keys to merge into the dependency's configuration file on top of the default config shipped in the archive.

config_file.description: Root-level archive file and game-directory target file used as the dependency config base.

installMapEntry.description: A single file mapping from the dependency archive to the game install directory, with integrity metadata.

dest.description: Target filename in the game directory (e.g. D3D9.dll).

tier.description: Specificity; higher wins. Conventionally id 100, fingerprint 90, exe-name 70, engine 40, generic 10.

defaults.description: Title defaults applied by the app when a title omits the field.

channel.description: Default curation-maturity channel. The generator derives stable for working titles and beta otherwise, then only emits a per-title channel when it differs from this default.

title.description: A catalogue entry — a game Luma is known to work with, matched by its rules and routed by `category` (default installable). min_app_version / channel are omitted when they equal the top-level defaults (the app merges). `status` defaults to `unknown` when omitted.

status.description: Upstream wiki test-map status: working = verified, construction = WIP/experimental, unknown = untested.

launch_args.description: Required launch arguments (e.g. -dx11, -nod3d9ex), shown to the user as a copyable callout rather than written automatically.

generic.description: Whether `asset` is a shared Generic-Mod build (Unreal Engine) curated onto this specific title, rather than a dedicated per-game build.
